channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: After meeting with Trump, Jordan's king says his country opposes displacing Palestinians in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted Jordan’s King Abdullah II at the White House on Tuesday and renewed his insistence that Gaza could somehow be emptied of all residents, controlled by the U.S. and redeveloped as a tourist area.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-FEMA worker who directed staff to avoid homes with Trump signs violated Hatch Act, agency says
item.description: A federal agency is seeking disciplinary action against a former Federal Emergency Management Agency worker who directed workers helping hurricane survivors to avoid homes with yard signs supporting President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained in Russia, has been released in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump prepares executive order to continue downsizing federal workforce
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sign an executive order on Tuesday that would continue downsizing the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: JD Vance rails against 'excessive' AI regulation in a rebuke to Europe at the Paris AI summit
item.description: PARIS (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance on Tuesday warned global leaders and tech industry executives that “excessive regulation” could cripple the rapidly growing artificial intelligence industry in a rebuke to European efforts to curb AI’s risks.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top political handicapper reveals what Dems' chances are at winning back the Senate in 2026
item.description: Democrats face “few opportunities" to win back the Senate majority from the Republicans in the 2026 midterm elections, a top non-partisan political handicapper predicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE cuts $900 million from agency that tracks American students' academic progress
item.description: A federal research office that tracks the progress of America’s students is being hit with almost $900 million in cuts after Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency found no need for much of its work.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Noem, Hegseth, Bondi plead with Congress for more border funding amid large-scale deportations
item.description: Newly sworn-in President Donald Trump Cabinet officials are requesting more resources from Congress to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York mayor vows to regain public's trust after Justice Department orders halt to prosecution
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams thanked the Justice Department for ordering a halt to his criminal corruption case and castigated the prosecution as “cruel” while vowing to regain the trust of voters ahead of his upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nearly half of NY voters don’t want Mayor Adams’ charges dropped: poll
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Nearly half of New Yorkers oppose Mayor Eric Adams’ charges being dropped, according to a recent PIX11 poll.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Murphy looks to be guiding force for Democrats in new Trump era
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) is taking a front-and-center role in the efforts to push Democrats toward becoming the party of economic populism and to challenge President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth renames North Carolina military base Fort Roland L. Bragg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has signed an order restoring the name of a storied special operations forces base back to Fort Bragg.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota nonprofits ride waves of tumult from Trump's spending freeze attempts
item.description: President Donald Trump’s federal spending freeze was recently blocked by a judge, but the tumult leaves Minnesota’s nearly 9,000 nonprofits and tax-exempt entities that receive federal money wondering what’s next.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gracie Stockton, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Trump steps up his 2018 tariffs on steel and aluminum, risking inflation on promise of more jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday removed the exceptions and exemptions from his 2018 tariffs on steel, meaning that all steel imports will be taxed at a minimum of 25%. Trump also hiked his 2018 aluminum tariffs to 25% from 10%.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Ohio State coach Jim Tressel joins a long list of sports figures who've moved into politics
item.description: College Football Hall of Fame coach Jim Tressel, who won the 2002 national title at Ohio State University, has been nominated to be the state’s next lieutenant governor, joining a long list of sports figures who’ve moved into politics.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Feb. 10 is the deadline to register to vote for 32nd Assembly District special election
item.description: The 32nd Assembly District special election is about two weeks away and registered voters in Kern and Tulare counties should have their vote-by-mail-ballots by now.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release billions of dollars in funding. The Trump administration quickly appealed the ruling. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump enacts steel and aluminum tariffs, pardons Blagojevich
item.description: Trump said the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked which countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Palestinians will have no right of return to Gaza under US proposal
item.description: No, they wouldn’t,” Trump declared. “In other words, I'm talking about building a permanent place for them.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinian refugees in Syria have a message for Gazans: Don’t leave your land
item.description: Palestinians fled the 1948 Mideast War and took refuge in neighboring Syria. After 77 years, they’re still waiting to go back. They are telling Palestinians in Gaza to stay put.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre and Jawad Rizkallah, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s two cents: President orders U.S. Treasury to ditch the penny to save money
item.description: President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Why many Republicans think shrinking Medicaid will make it better
item.description: Republicans proposals to change the public health insurance program for low-income and disabled Americans could amount to more than $2 trillion of cuts over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Levi, MPR News

item.title: Florida could ban these chemical additives in foods by 2028 if bill passes
item.description: Florida could ban chemical additives in foods by 2028 under a new bill that was filed on Monday by a Southwest Florida lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The EEOC exists to fight discrimination. Fired official fears a hobbling under Trump
item.description: Jocelyn Samuels was Trump's pick in 2020 to fill a Democratic seat on the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission. She was fired in January, accused of embracing "radical" ideology.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News
